channel.title: yourfdc.au News — Family Day Care Australia
channel.description: Australia's dedicated Family Day Care news hub.

item.title: G8 Education faces Federal Court action over more than $2 million in alleged underpayments
item.description: Australia’s largest for-profit early learning operator, G8 Education Limited, is facing Federal Court proceedings after the Fair Work Ombudsman alleged underpayments and compensation claims totalling more than $2 million for more than 1,400 workers.
item.content:encoded: Australia’s largest for-profit early learning operator, G8 Education Limited, is facing Federal Court proceedings after the Fair Work Ombudsman alleged underpayments and compensation claims totalling more than $2 million for more than 1,400 workers.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Extend receives $37,500 penalty after children left unsupervised during OSHC excursion
item.description: The State Administrative Tribunal of Western Australia has ordered Extend (Australia) Pty Ltd, trading as Marmion Primary School OSHC-Extend, to pay a $37,500 penalty after finding the provider breached supervision and child safety requirements under the Education and Care Services National Law (WA).
item.content:encoded: The State Administrative Tribunal of Western Australia has ordered Extend (Australia) Pty Ltd, trading as Marmion Primary School OSHC-Extend, to pay a $37,500 penalty after finding the provider breached supervision and child safety requirements under the Education and Care Services National Law (WA).
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: From the GFC to the cost-of-living crisis: what history teaches us about protecting ECEC occupancy
item.description: The early childhood education and care sector has navigated economic uncertainty before. During the Global Financial Crisis, providers learnt that occupancy is influenced by more than enrolment numbers, it is shaped by family confidence, workforce participation, affordability and trust. As cost-of-living pressures continue in 2026, those lessons remain highly relevant.
item.content:encoded: The early childhood education and care sector has navigated economic uncertainty before. During the Global Financial Crisis, providers learnt that occupancy is influenced by more than enrolment numbers, it is shaped by family confidence, workforce participation, affordability and trust. As cost-of-living pressures continue in 2026, those lessons remain highly relevant.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Early foundations, future pathways: why senior secondary success begins in early childhood
item.description: The Australian Government has released further information about the Preschool Outcomes Measure (POM as preparations continue for a national pilot during 2026 and 2027, providing early childhood teachers and educators with a new evidence-based tool to support children's learning and development in the year before school.
item.content:encoded: The Australian Government has released further information about the Preschool Outcomes Measure (POM as preparations continue for a national pilot during 2026 and 2027, providing early childhood teachers and educators with a new evidence-based tool to support children's learning and development in the year before school.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.content:encoded: South Australia’s Flying Start Early Childhood Awards celebrate excellence across the sector - The Sector
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Australia reaches six-month paid parental leave: what it means for the early childhood sector - The Sector
item.description: Australia reaches six-month paid parental leave: what it means for the early childhood sector - The Sector
item.content:encoded: Australia reaches six-month paid parental leave: what it means for the early childhood sector - The Sector
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: UK childcare investigation puts affordability, transparency and sustainability under the microscope
item.description: The UK’s Competition and Markets Authority has launched a childcare sector investigation examining how the market is working for families, providers and the wider economy. While the review is UK-based, its focus on affordability, availability, transparency and sustainability raises questions that will feel familiar across Australia’s early childhood education and care sector.
item.content:encoded: The UK’s Competition and Markets Authority has launched a childcare sector investigation examining how the market is working for families, providers and the wider economy. While the review is UK-based, its focus on affordability, availability, transparency and sustainability raises questions that will feel familiar across Australia’s early childhood education and care sector.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Goodstart investigates Payneham centre incident after child exits service
item.description: Goodstart Early Learning has confirmed an internal investigation is underway after a child followed a grandparent out of its Payneham centre last month.
item.content:encoded: Goodstart Early Learning has confirmed an internal investigation is underway after a child followed a grandparent out of its Payneham centre last month.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Everyone experiences loss and grief – but that doesn’t make it any easier to talk with kids about it
item.description: Parents and educators sometimes avoid having conversations about grief with children because they don’t know what to say. Here are some tips for how to approach it.
item.content:encoded: Parents and educators sometimes avoid having conversations about grief with children because they don’t know what to say. Here are some tips for how to approach it.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: The Breadwinner: Comedy doesn’t need flawless dads, but we do need competent ones
item.description: Comedic portrayals of incompetent dads can distort public understanding of fathers’ essential and real-life contributions to children’s development.
item.content:encoded: Comedic portrayals of incompetent dads can distort public understanding of fathers’ essential and real-life contributions to children’s development.

item.title: Childcare workers have been guaranteed a pay bump. What’s the fine print?
item.description: The federal government has set aside $3.6 billion over the next two years for early educator pay. But what happens after that is not yet clear.
item.content:encoded: The federal government has set aside $3.6 billion over the next two years for early educator pay. But what happens after that is not yet clear.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Toy Story 5 pits traditional toys vs a tablet. In real life, families can combine the two
item.description: The latest instalment of the film franchise addresses a dilemma many parents face: what happens to playtime when children become obsessed with screens?
